megacolonies.quests.general.alchemy.obj0: I just discovered a new alchemical process but I need some materials to test it out. Do you think you could help me with that?
Splendid! Well, I require the flesh of three zombies!
I suddenly don't feel like being part of this.
I'm sorry, I can't help right now.
I won't be able to help you with that.
megacolonies.quests.general.alchemy.obj1: *Laughs Maniacally* I have done it. I have done it! I have created a potion of eternal life! Here try it!
megacolonies.quests.general.alchemy.obj2: The flesh you delivered me seemed a bit cold. I feel I require warm zombie flesh. Three again please
Uhhh, I get to kill some more zombies. Sure!
I suddenly don't feel like being part of this anymore.
megacolonies.quests.general.alchemy.obj5: Splendid! This looks just perfect! But I'm afraid I need something new! Fetch me 2 fresh spider eyes.
megacolonies.quests.general.alchemy.obj7: Hehehehehe! Perfect! Now I just need something to fuel the reaction. Gun powder would be perfect!
I have no concern. No problem!
One creeper for the gun powder or stealing some from the guards? No problem!
This sounds concerning. I want out!
megacolonies.quests.general.alchemy.obj9: Splendid! Let me mix them together real quick!
megacolonies.quests.general.cookies.obj0: Hi, Thank you for hiring me as a baker! I always wanted to be a baker! Let's make some cookies for young ones!
I need two wheat and one coco bean!
They're not my kids, why should I do it?
I'm sorry, I can't help right now.
I won't be able to help you with that.
megacolonies.quests.general.cookies.obj3: Thank you. I'll get right on this!
megacolonies.quests.general.cookies.obj4: Here you are. Please take those 8 cookies and deliver them to $1! You can find the kid with the help of the quest log.
megacolonies.quests.general.cookies.obj5: Hi! The baker told me you got cookies for me!
Yes, But I ate them all!
You are a so mean! I will never forget this!
Yes, I have them here! Give me a second!
megacolonies.quests.general.cookies.obj7: Please make sure to share them with your friends!
Suuuure! I found this shiny thing on the floor. Here you can have it!
Thank you! I found this shiny thing on the floor. Here you can have it!
Thank you! I found this shiny thing on the floor. Here you can have it!
megacolonies.quests.general.hungrycourie: Hey there, could I ask you a favour?
I'm starving and I forgot my lunch at home. Could you bring me some food?
What kind of food do you want?
A baked potato would just hit the spot!
Got it. I'll be right back.
I'm sorry, I don't have any food on me.
Ah, too bad. I guess I'll just have to make do until I get home.
I'll see if I can find something for you later.
Sorry, I'm in a rush right now!
Oh, this was great! Thanks a lot!
megacolonies.quests.general.thezombiemen: We have a problem. The Zombies in the nearby forest are causing trouble. Can you help us take care of them?
Sure, I'll do what I can.
Thank you! We need you to kill 2 Zombies. We'll reward you for your efforts.
I'm sorry, I can't help right now.
You did it! We're safe for now. Here's your reward.
We have a problem. The Zombies in the nearby forest are causing trouble. Can you help us take care of them?
Sure, I'll do what I can.
Thank you! We need you to kill 10 Zombies. We'll reward you for your efforts.
I'm sorry, I can't help right now.
You did it! We're safe for now. Here's your reward.
megacolonies.quests.guides.buildergoggle: Hey there! Can I ask you for a favor? I lost my glasses the other day and I can't see very well without them. Do you think you could get me a new pair of builders goggles?
Of course, I'll see what I can do!
Sorry, I don't have time to help you right now.
I already know all about the builder goggles. You'll be fine without them!
Ah, you found my glasses! Thank you so much. I can finally see clearly what I'm building again. You should try them out sometime, they're pretty neat.
megacolonies.quests.guides.clipboard.obj: Hey there! Have you heard of the clipboard? It's a tool that can help you keep track of all the requests in our colony. If you could bring me one, I would be forever grateful!
Sure, I'll see what I can do!
Sorry, I can't help you right now.
I already know what it does!

Great, you found a clipboard! To use it, simply shift-right click it on any hut block. This will give you an overview of all the requests in the colony. It's super handy, and I am so grateful that you were able to get one for me. Thank you!
megacolonies.quests.guides.rallybanner.o: Lately, we've been seeing a lot of skeletons wandering around at night. If you could help us out by taking care of ten of them, we'd be very grateful!
No problem, I'll take care of it!
Sorry, I'm too busy right now.
Sorry, I don't think I can help you with that.
Thank you for taking care of those skeletons! You're very brave. Listen, I have something that might help us out. It's called a rallying banner. Could you bring it to me?
Sorry, I don't think I can help you with that.
A rallying banner? Sounds interesting. I'll go find it.

Thanks for bringing me the rallying banner! To use it, simply shift-right click it on any hut block. This will allow you to assign guards to it, and you can manage it by right clicking in the air. It's a great tool for keeping the colony safe!
Great, I'll make sure to put it to good use!
megacolonies.quests.guides.resourcescrol: Hello there! Have you heard about the Resource Scroll? It's a powerful tool that can help you gather resources more efficiently. I really need one, but I don't have the time to go and find it myself. Would you be willing to bring one to me?
Sure, I can try to find one for you!
I'm sorry, I don't have the time right now.
I already know what it does!

Thank you so much for bringing me the Resource Scroll! You know, all you have to do is shift-right-click it on the hut block to activate it. It will help you gather resources more quickly and efficiently. I hope you find it as useful as I do!
Thanks for the tip, I'll be sure to use it!
megacolonies.quests.romance.aromanticges: Hello there, could I ask you to help me with something?
I want to give a poppy to $1, but I'm too shy to do it myself. Can you deliver it for me?
Of course, I'll be happy to help.
I'm sorry, but I can't do that for you.
Oh, that's too bad. I guess I'll have to try to do it myself.
I'm sorry, I don't have time right now.
Oh, this is beautiful! Thank you so much! Who is it from?
Oh, I see! I'll be sure to thank them. Here, take this as a token of my gratitude.
Hey, thank you so much for delivering that poppy to me earlier. It really made my day. I was wondering if I could ask you for another favour?
Of course, what do you need?
I have a friend who is feeling a bit down and I want to cheer them up. Could you bring $1 this gift for me?
Sure, I'll be happy to help.
I'm sorry, but I'm a bit busy right now.
Oh, that's too bad. Maybe another time then.
Sure, I'll keep it in mind.
I'm sorry, but I can't help you right now.
Oh, hello! $0 told me to bring you a gift to cheer you up.
How kind of them! Thank you so much!
Thank you so much for delivering that gift. You're a true friend.
No problem, glad I could help.
megacolonies.quests.template.questtempla: Could you bring me an Apple? I'm really hungry!
Sorry, I don't have time right now!
We don't have any apples right now!
Could I bring you a baked potato?
I don't have time right now!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.builder.obj: Hey there! I saw you got your builder hut placed. Nice! Are you ready for the next step?
Yes sure!
Nice, so let's get this builder hut built then. Right click the builder hut block, go into 'Build Options' and click 'Build Building'
Already done!
On it!

No Problem! To see what the builder requires for this building you can right click the hut and select the 'Required Resources' tab which is the second tab from the top on the left. You can drop off items there directly or through the inventory available through the inventory button in the bottom middle of the main page or by shift right clicking the block.
Okay thanks!
Great! Come back to me once you checked that out!
Understood, be right back!
I already finished building the hut!
Oh nice, then we are already done here and I will have more information on the next steps soon!
Awesome!
Not right now. I will come back to you later!

No, I forgot what I had to do!
No Problem! Right click the builder hut block, go into 'Build Options' and click 'Build Building'
Thanks! Will try again!
Yes, done, what's next?

Nice! To see what the builder requires for this building you can right click the hut and select the 'Required Resources' tab which is the second tab from the top on the left. You can drop off items there directly or through the inventory by shift right clicking on the block.
Okay thanks!
Great! Come back to me once you checked that out!

No Problem! To see what the builder requires for this building you can right click the hut and select the 'Required Resources' tab which is the second tab from the top on the left. You can drop off items there directly or through the inventory available through the inventory button in the bottom middle of the main page or by shift right clicking the block.
Okay thanks!
Great! Come back to me once you checked that out!
Understood, be right back!

Awesome! Your builder might also need some tools to prepare the building area. You can supervise your builders immediate needs by right clicking them and selecting the 'Open Requests' tab which is the second from the top on the left. You can fulfill the request right there or through their inventory by shift right clicking them.
Oh nice, good to know!
Great! Come back to me once the builder finished building.
On it!
I already finished building the hut!
Oh nice, then we are already done here and I will have more information on the next steps soon!
Awesome!
Not yet, I will get to it later!
Congratulations. You built the first building of your colony! Come back to me in a bit and I will let you know the next steps! Here, have some cobblestone as a reward!
Thank you!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.builder2.ob: I see your colony is growing nicely. How about we upgrade your builders hut to take your colony to the next level?

Upgrading buildings yields many benefits. Aside from your citizens leveling up faster they may also use higher tool tiers. You can upgrade the building through the build options after right clicking the hut block.
Excellent. I'll leave you be for now then. Make sure to check on your quest log regularly.
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
Excellent. I'll leave you be for now then. Make sure to check on your quest log regularly.

Awesome! This will now also allow you to upgrade all kinds of other buildings. If you want to construct multiple buildings in parallel, consider getting a second builder. Make sure to regularly check the quest log for future tasks!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.farm.obj0: Hello there! We are producing some wood, but you might have already noticed that us citizens also require food, so we should try to build a farm so that the colony can also be self sufficient on food.
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.farm.obj0.a: Nice, so as usual we start by crafting the farm hut block. In the crafting bench, surround a wooden or stone hoe with planks and put the Build Tool in the top. Come back to me once you've done that!'
Alrighty then! Then it's time to place the Farm with the Build Tool, click build and provide your builder with the necessary items like the last time and come back once it is built!
I already set up the farm!
Oh nice, then we are already done here and I will have more information on the next steps soon!
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.farm.obj1: Welcome back, were you able to craft the Farm hut block?
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.farm.obj1.a: A great leap forward often requires taking two steps back! In the crafting bench, surround a wooden or stone hoe with planks and put the Build Tool in the top.
Alright! I'll get back to it!
Alrighty then! Then it's time to place the Farm with the Build Tool, click build and provide your builder with the necessary items like the last time and come back once it is built!
Not yet, I will get to it later!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.farm.obj3: Okay great. But before your farmer can start working they need a field. To craft it in the crafting bench put a pumpkin or hay bale at the top, leather in the middle and then sticks to the right and left and below the the leather.
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.farm.obj3.a: I already set up the field!
Oh nice, then we are already done here and I will have more information on the next steps soon!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.farm.obj4: Welcome back, were you able to craft the Field block?
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.farm.obj4.a: A great leap forward often requires taking two steps back! In the crafting bench, put a pumpkin or hay bale at the top, leather in the middle and then sticks to the right and left and below the the leather.
Alright! I'll get back to it!

Great! You can either manually place the field by right clicking the block close to the farm in some open space. Or let the Builder build a pre-made field from the build tool menu. Either way, make sure there is water for the field. Come back to me once either of that is done!
Not yet, I will get to it later!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.farm.obj6: Now all that is missing is setting a crop type for your farmer to plant by right clicking the field block and selecting a type of crop. Here have some seeds, I'll let you know about the next steps soon!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.forester.ob: Hello my friend! I see you got the tavern up and running. I think this is a good time to get a Forester to make sure your colony produces enough Wood!
Yes sure!

Nice, so as usual we start by crafting the Forester hut block. In the crafting bench, surround a wooden or stone axe with planks and put the Build Tool in the top. Come back to me once you've done that!'
On it!
Already done!
Alrighty then! Then it's time to place the Forester with the Build Tool, click build and provide your Builder with the necessary items like the last time and come back once it is built!
Consider it done!
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
Already done!
Oh nice, then we are done here and I will have more information on the next steps soon!
Nice!
Welcome back, were you able to craft the Forester hut block?
No, I forgot the recipe!
A great leap forward often requires taking two steps back! In the crafting bench, surround a wooden or stone axe with planks and put the Build Tool in the top.
Alright! I'll get back to it!

Alrighty then! Then it's time to place the Forester with the Build Tool, click build and provide your Builder with the necessary items like the last time and come back once it is built!
Consider it done!

Neat, soon your colony will be mostly autonomous on wood. Make sure to check in on your forester now and then to make sure they got the tools they need and you can grow some different types of trees around the hut to get different types of logs, Come back in a bit and I'll help you with the next steps! Here, have some torches, those will sure come in handy!
See you in a bit!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.hospital.ob: Now that the university is finished, our next concern is our healthcare. We should really get a hospital in this town.
The doctors in the hospital will heal your citizens from all kinds of diseases, making it an essential part of your colony! But before we do that, we need to first research it at the university!'
Alrighty then! You know the drill, craft the hospital hut block in a crafting bench. This time surround a carrot with planks and the build tool at the top.
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
Oh nice, then we are done here and I will have more information on the next steps soon!

Now that the research is done, the process is the same. Craft and place the hut, then let the builder build it! You know the drill, craft the hospital hut block in a crafting bench. This time surround a carrot with planks and the build tool at the top.
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
Awesome. I feel much better now knowing there is a hospital in the colony! Here, take this for an initial stock of medicine in the hospital!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.housing.obj: Now that all your 4 basic citizens have a job, it's time to think about expanding the colony to get more citizens. From this point on, you will need open housing space to get more citizens either by hiring visitors from the tavern or waiting for your citizens to have children!
I'm all ears!

Residences offer one housing spot per building level. So, let's start by crafting 4 Residence hut blocks. In the crafting bench, surround a torch with planks and put the Build Tool in the top. Come back to me once you've done that!'
On it!
Already done!
Perfect! Then it's time to place the 4 Residences with the Build Tool, click build and provide your Builder with the necessary items like the last time and come back once all 4 are built!
Consider it done!
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
Already done!
Oh nice, then we are done here and I will have more information on the next steps soon!
Nice!
Welcome back, were you able to craft the 4 Residence hut blocks?
No, I forgot the recipe!
A great leap forward often requires taking two steps back! In the crafting bench, surround a torch with planks and put the Build Tool in the top.
Alright! I'll get back to it!
Yes, done, what's next?
Perfect! Then it's time to place the 4 Residences with the Build Tool, click build and provide your Builder with the necessary items like the last time and come back once all 4 are built!
Consider it done!
Not yet, I will get to it later!

Awesome! Now you should have some space to hire some visitors from the tavern (by right clicking and providing the items they require) or at least have space for your citizens to have some children. Here, have some wool, it will sure come in handy. Will contact you in a bit again!
See you in a bit!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.housing2.ob: Now that you got a level 2 builder the next logical step is upgrading your residences. Residences house 1 citizen per housing level up to five citizens at level five.
Sounds exciting! What do you want me to do?.
Upgrade two of your residences to level 2 and come back to me later then!
Excellent. I'll leave you be for now then. Make sure to check on your quest log regularly.
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
Excellent. I'll leave you be for now then. Make sure to check on your quest log regularly.

Amazing! This is the best way to attract new citizens to your colony! Not only that! Citizens are also considerably happier in higher residence tiers! Here I found these racks earlier. I'm sure they will come in handy!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.military.ba: The defences are starting to look good. But we're still lacking some offensive power in the case of a large scale invasion. We should get some barracks up and running!

Barracks come with up to 4 barrack towers (at level 4+) where each tower can house up to 5 guards (at level 5) giving you a whopping 20 guards! But before we do that, we need to first research it at the university!'
Alrighty then! You know the drill, craft the barracks hut block in a crafting bench. This time surround a block of iron with planks and the build tool at the top.
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
Oh nice, then we are done here and I will have more information on the next steps soon!

Now that the research is done, the process is the same. Craft and place the hut, then let the builder build it! You know the drill, craft the barracks hut block in a crafting bench. This time surround a block of iron with planks and the build tool at the top.
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
Great. If you want to start having guards you should also build and upgrade the barracks towers. I will leave this to you. Here, some equipment to get your guards going!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.military.gu: Building Defences
I'm still not feeling very safe. Some might argue that the strongest defense is a swift and decisive offense. How about you build some guard towers?
Alright, on it!
Nice, so as usual we start by crafting the Guard Tower block. In the crafting bench, surround a bow with planks and put the Build Tool in the top. Come back to me once you've done that!'
On it!
Already done!
Alrighty then! Then it's time to place both Guard Towers with the Build Tool, click build and provide your Builder with the necessary items like the last time and come back once they are built!
Consider it done!
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
Already done!
Oh nice, then we are done here and I will have more information on the next steps soon!
Nice!
Welcome back, were you able to craft the Guard Tower block?
No, I forgot the recipe!
A great leap forward often requires taking two steps back! In the crafting bench, surround a bow with planks and put the Build Tool in the top.
Alright! I'll get back to it!
Yes, done, what's next?
Alrighty then! Then it's time to place the Guard Towers with the Build Tool, click build and provide your Builder with the necessary items like the last time and come back once both are built!
Consider it done!
I already finished building the Guard Towers!
Oh nice, then we are already done here and I will have more information on the next steps soon!
Awesome!
Not yet, I will get to it later!

Awesome, I feel much safer already. Guards actually live in their guard towers and don't need housing spots. So just make sure they have armor and weapons and they will serve you well. Come back in a bit and I'll help you with the next steps!
See you in a bit!
Expanding your Territory
I see your colony is coming together nicely. I believe it'd be a good idea to place and upgrade some guard towers to expand the colony borders and bolster your defences!
Sure, sounds good!
Excellent, then go craft some more guard tower blocks, place them and have your builder build and upgrade them. Let's target around 10 levels of guard towers. That's like 5 level 2 guard towers.'
On it!
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
Awesome, your village is expanding and ready to face intruders. Come back in a bit and I'll help you with the next steps! Here, have a set of armor to equip your guards with!
See you in a bit!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.military.to: Hello! I see your colony is growing. However, it is quite dangerous around here. How about you light the area up a little bit to avoid monsters preying on your citizens?
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
Great, thanks! This should definitely help a bit. Here, I found these, I'm sure they will come in handy!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.military.zo: Hi, there seem to be a lot of dangerous mobs around here! Would you mind killing some zombies to make us feel more at ease?
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
Great, thanks! I feel more at ease already. Here, have some leather armor!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.mine.obj0: Hello there! We should be producing enough food for now. Just make sure your colonist stay fed by leaving food in their inventories. The next step is then to get a Mine up and running!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.mine.obj0.a: So, as usual we start by crafting the Mine hut block. In the crafting bench, surround a wooden or stone pickaxe with planks and put the Build Tool in the top. Come back to me once you've done that!'
Alrighty then! Then it's time to place the Mine with the Build Tool, click build and provide your Builder with the necessary items like the last time and come back once it is built!
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
Oh nice, then we are done here and I will have more information on the next steps soon!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.mine.obj1: Welcome back, were you able to craft the Mine hut block?
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.mine.obj1.a: A great leap forward often requires taking two steps back! In the crafting bench, surround a wooden or stone pickaxe with planks and put the Build Tool in the top.
Alright! I'll get back to it!
Alrighty then! Then it's time to place the Mine with the Build Tool, click build and provide your Builder with the necessary items like the last time and come back once it is built!
I already finished building the Mine!
Oh nice, then we are already done here and I will have more information on the next steps soon!
Not yet, I will get to it later!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.mine.obj3: Neat, soon your miner will start bringing in all kinds of stones and ores, just make sure that you provide them the necessary tools and materials. The miner will first dig down and build a shaft structure for which they will require some material. Later they will also expand horizontally.
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.mine.obj3.a: Come back in a bit and I'll help you with the next steps! Here, have some ladders, the miner will need them soon!
While we are getting food from the farm, you still have to manually feed the citizens when they are hungry. How about we build a dining hall so that is taken care of automatically?
Sounds great!!

The waiter in the dining hall will not only cook food for you in the furnace automatically, but they will also hand out food to citizens that are hungry. You know the drill, craft the dining hall hut block in a crafting bench. This time surround an apple with planks and the build tool at the top.'
On it!
Already done!
Alrighty then! Then it's time to place the dining hall with the Build Tool, click build and provide your Builder with the necessary items like the last time and come back once it is built!
Consider it done!
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
Already done!
Oh nice, then we are done here and I will have more information on the next steps soon!
Nice!
Welcome back, were you able to craft the dining hall hut block?
No, I forgot the recipe!
No problem! In the crafting bench, surround an apple with planks and put the Build Tool in the top.
Alright! I'll get back to it!
Yes, done, what's next?
Alrighty then! Then it's time to place the dining hall with the Build Tool, click build and provide your Builder with the necessary items like the last time and come back once it is built!
Consider it done!
Not yet, I will get to it later!

Neat, soon the waiter will hand out food to your citizens, just make sure the dining hall is supplied with food and there is a menu setup for what the waiter will hand out. Keep in mind that vanilla foods are not as saturating for citizens as they are for players. Here, have some coal to serve as initial fuel!
See you in a bit!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.sawmill.obj: I see you working yourself to death crafting all these stairs and planks on your own. I think it's about time to get a sawmill to automate this task!

The sawmill is able to craft most wood related blocks on demand and your couriers will make sure that they get all they need for it! But before we do that, we need to first research it at the university!'

Alrighty then! You know the drill, craft the sawmill hut block in a crafting bench, place it and let your builder build it. This time put three wooden axes in the middle, put the build tool at the top and fill up the rest with planks.
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
Oh nice, then we are done here and I will have more information on the next steps soon!

Alrighty then! You know the drill, craft the sawmill hut block in a crafting bench, place it and let your builder build it. This time put three wooden axes in the middle, put the build tool at the top and fill up the rest with planks.
Not right now. I will come back to you later!

Great, the sawmill has a recipe page where you can teach all the recipes you want the sawmill to handle automatically. You can upgrade the hut to unlock more recipe slots, and research and build the other Crafters in a similar fashion.
This is going to be so useful!
I have taught you everything I know. and you have become a far greater Mayor than I could ever hope to be. Good luck on this journey!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.tavern.obj0: Hi there! Your builder is looking neat! How about we let them build a Tavern so that your citizens don't have to sleep outside and are safe at night?
Nice, so as usual we start by crafting the Tavern hut block. In the crafting bench, surround a Barrel with planks and put the Build Tool in the top. Come back to me once you've done that!'
Alrighty then! Then it's time to place the Tavern with the Build Tool, click build and provide your Builder with the necessary items like the last time and come back once it is built!
Oh nice, then we are done here and I will have more information on the next steps soon!
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.tavern.obj1: Welcome back, were you able to craft the tavern hut block?
A great leap forward often requires taking two steps back! Just surround a Barrel with planks in the crafting table and put the Build Tool in the top middle slot.
Alright! I'll get back to it!
Alrighty then! Then it's time to place the Tavern with the Build Tool, click build and provide your Builder with the necessary items like the last time and come back once it is built!
I already finished building the Tavern!
Oh nice, then we are already done here and I will have more information on the next steps soon!
Not yet, I will get to it later!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.tavern.obj3: Amazing, you are now providing housing for all your citizens. The Tavern provides four housing slots but you can have at most one Tavern per colony. Come back a bit later and I will let you know the next steps! Here, have some baked potatoes as a reward to help keep the citizens fed!
Hi, I've seen a fancy new residence was just recently completed. The people that live there start becoming a bit more picky with food.
Uhh, eehh, what does that mean?
First, aside from the dining hall you should build a Chef's Kitchen that is able to cook some of the special dishes and provide them to the restaurant!
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
I know all about that already!
Excellent. I'll leave you be for now then. Make sure to check on your quest log regularly.

Great, now that the Chef's Kitchen is finished, make sure that your farmer grows the crops for the recipes. You can obtain the seeds from breaking blocks like grass, fern, bushes, etc with a hoe! Here, have some Onion and Garlic to get started!
Forgive me for interrupting, Your Highness. I meant no disrespect. But now that we have a warehouse and a courier, I'm sure you would like to know how to automate crafting.
Unfortunately, at the moment your colony still lacks the know-how. But don't falter, this is the perfect moment to get a university to acquire all the necessary knowledge.
I will do what I must!

Excellent. Craft the University hut block by putting a book in the middle and bottom slot of the crafting grid, surround it with planks and put a build tool in the top. Then find a suitable location in the colony to place it and let your builder build it to level 1.
Excellent. I'll leave you be for now then. Make sure to check on your quest log regularly.
Excellent. I'll leave you be for now then. Make sure to check on your quest log regularly.
Not right now. I will come back to you later!

Amazing. The university is the gateway to a rich selection of technologies that allow you to expand, grow and automate your colony. Upgrading the building unlocks additional research possibilities and allows you to hire more researchers.
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.warehouse.o: Hi! I'm sure your feet are hurting by now because you are manually fulfilling all the citizens needs! I got good news! You can automate most of this with the help of a warehouse and courier!
Tell me more about it!

Let's start by crafting a warehouse block by surrounding a chest with planks and the build tool in the top slot and a courier hut by surrounding a set of chain boots with planks and the build tool in the top slot.
On it!
Already got a warehouse and courier!
Excellent. I'll leave you be for now then. Make sure to check on your quest log regularly.
Noted!
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
Already got one!!
Excellent. I'll leave you be for now then. Make sure to check on your quest log regularly.
Noted!
Welcome back, did you craft the warehouse and courier hut blocks already?
No, I forgot what I had to do!
Craft a warehouse block by surrounding a chest with planks and the build tool in the top slot and a courier hut by surrounding a set of chain boots with planks and the build tool in the top slot.
On it!
Not right now. I will come back to you later!
Yes! Got them right here!!

That was fast! Next place the buildings with the help of the build tool, click build and supply your builder with the materials. I advise you to place the warehouse somewhat centrally in your colony for easy access. The courier hut location is inconsequential on the other hand.
Noted!

Neat! The food your farmer produces will now automatically reach the dining hall through the warehouse. Here, have a set of tools you can drop off in the warehouse to be delivered to your citizens on demand.
Thank you. Bye!
megacolonies.quests.tutorial.welcome.obj: Hello there! I just arrived here and wondered if you wish any guidance setting up the colony?
Yes please!

So let's start by picking a style for your colony first! You can pick a unique style for your colony by going to your Town Hall and right clicking the block. This is the main page. Now, on the right hand side, click the button next to 'Colony Pack' to select your colony style.
Alright! Let me get to it!
I already picked a style!

Okay great. Next, I suggest you to browse the Build Tool to find different buildings you can build. With the Build Tool in your hand, right click on the ground, try to locate the Builder's Hut, then come back to me!
Okay, let me do that!
Okay, I'll do that again!
Yes, but not now. I will come back to you later!
I'm a Colony Master! You underestimate my power!
Hi! Did you choose a colony style already?
No, I forgot what I had to do!

No Problem! You can pick a unique style for your colony by going to your Town Hall and right clicking the block. This is the main page. Now, on the right hand side, click the button next to 'Colony Pack' to select your colony style.
Thanks! Let me check again!
Yes, all done, what is next?
Okay great. Next, I suggest you browse the Build Tool a little bit. With the Build Tool in your hand, right click on the ground, try to locate the Builder's Hut, then come back to me!
Okay, let me do that!
Okay, I'll do that again!
Not yet, I will get to it later!
Welcome back! Were you able to navigate the Build Tool?
Yes; mission accomplished!

Awesome! Then, before placing it, we first have to craft a Builder's Hut hut block. Just surround a door with planks in the crafting table and put the Build Tool in the middle top slot. Come back to me after you have crafted the hut block!
Alright! Let me get to it!
No, what did I have to do again?
Skill is the child of patience. Browse the Build Tool a little bit. With the Build Tool in your hand, right click on the ground, try to locate the Builder's Hut, then come back to me!
Sure! I'll try!
Not yet, but I will come back to you later!
Welcome back! Did you craft the hut block already?
No, I forgot the recipe!
A great leap forward often requires taking two steps back! Just surround a door with planks in the crafting table and put the Build Tool in the top slot.
Alright! Let me get to it!
Yes, all done. What's next?

Perfect! The next step is placing it with the help of the Build Tool. Right click the Build Tool on the ground. Navigate to the Builder's Hut (typically in the fundamentals category) and click on Builder's Hut. Then, you can select a level to preview and in some cases also different alternatives. Use the arrow buttons to adjust the position, rotation, and mirroring. Finally, click the checkmark to place it.
Alrighty then! Let me check this out!
Already done!
Not yet, I will get to it later!
Perfect! The building is placed, and a worker will start working there soon. Take this quest log and shift+right-click it on any of the hut blocks in the colony to stay updated with the next steps!
Perfect! Thanks!
advancements.megamod.army_30.description: 30 soldiers makes for a prestigious colony
advancements.megamod.army_30.title: Lieutenant
advancements.megamod.army_8.description: It's only 8 guards, but your army must start somewhere
advancements.megamod.army_8.title: §oDecanus
advancements.megamod.army_80.description: An army of 80 is not a small obstacle
advancements.megamod.army_80.title: Centurion
advancements.megamod.build.all_towers.de: A mighty barracks to strike fear in the enemy - all upgrades, all towers
advancements.megamod.build.archery.descr: Give your Knights some backup. Build an Archery to train Archers.
advancements.megamod.build.archery.title: Bullseye
advancements.megamod.build.baker.descrip: Your citizens need some grains in their diet. Build a Bakery so the Baker can craft baked goods for them!
advancements.megamod.build.baker.title: A Legend in the Baking
advancements.megamod.build.barracks.desc: You're going to need many more Guards. Build a Barracks to hire a lot more in a much smaller space!
advancements.megamod.build.barracks_towe: Build the Barracks Towers inside your new Barracks. These towers are locked to the same level as the Barracks and will increase their max Guards every level!
advancements.megamod.build.blacksmith.de: Most citizens need some form of tool. Build the Blacksmith's Hut to allow automated crafting of tools!
advancements.megamod.build.builder.descr: Keep your Builder fed and fulfill their requests until they finish building your first hut
advancements.megamod.build.builder.title: Build a Builder Building
advancements.megamod.build.chicken_herde: Build a Chicken Farmer's Hut to bring in some eggs and meat. Remember to bring the chickens to the hut.
advancements.megamod.build.citizen.descr: Your citizens need a place to live! Build them a House - each level houses one citizen.
advancements.megamod.build.citizen.title: Home Sweet Home
advancements.megamod.build.combat_academ: Battle is dangerous. Build a Combat Academy and assign a citizen to train there without the risk of losing their life!
advancements.megamod.build.composter.des: A Composter will take your leftover saplings, leaves, and like items and expose them to the elements to make fertilizer.
advancements.megamod.build.cook.descript: Build a dining hall to give your citizens a place to get food. The Waiter will cook meats and give food to you and your citizens.
advancements.megamod.build.cook.title: Feeding the Masses
advancements.megamod.build.cowboy.descri: Build a Cowhand's Hut to bring in that beef. Remember to bring the cows to your Cowhand.
advancements.megamod.build.cowboy.title: Yeeehaw!
advancements.megamod.build.crusher.descr: Build a Crusher's Hut to get things like gravel from cobblestone!
advancements.megamod.build.crusher.title: A Crushing Feeling
advancements.megamod.build.delivery_pers: Build a Courier's Hut and hire a Courier so they can fulfill worker requests automatically!
advancements.megamod.build.enchanter.des: The Enchanter's Tower will consume ancient tomes and other workers' experience to produce enchanted books
advancements.megamod.build.farmer.descri: To start food production for your citizens, build a Farm. You'll need to place the Field (scarecrow) to mark where your Farmer will farm.
advancements.megamod.build.farmer.title: Have a Field Day
advancements.megamod.build.fisherman.des: Build a Fisher's Hut close to water to bring in those deep sea morsels
advancements.megamod.build.florist.descr: The Flower Shop will use compost or other types of "bonemeal" to grow flowers.
advancements.megamod.build.florist.title: Floral Arrangements
advancements.megamod.build.guard_tower.d: Build a Guard Tower and hire a Guard to protect against others who may begin to notice your growing town!
advancements.megamod.build.herders.descr: Build one of every herder hut. Remember to bring animals to their huts!
advancements.megamod.build.herders.title: Unherd Of
advancements.megamod.build.library.descr: If you want more skilled labor, you'd best build a Library to educate your citizens!
advancements.megamod.build.library.title: Higher Learning
advancements.megamod.build.lumberjack.de: Complete building a Forester's Hut (use your build tool to place it) to collect wood for more huts
advancements.megamod.build.miner.descrip: Complete building a Mine (use your build tool to place it) to get some ores and cobble for your huts
advancements.megamod.build.miner.title: Build the Mine
A Mystical Site may be pointless. It will only make your citizens proud. But you want one, so you should research and build one!
advancements.megamod.build.mysticalsite_: Upgrade your Mystical Site to level 5. Become the envy of the whole world!
I can see my house from here!
advancements.megamod.build.sawmill.descr: With the Courier by your side, you can utilise crafters! Build the Sawmill to start your crafting experience.
advancements.megamod.build.sawmill.title: A Saw Point
advancements.megamod.build.shepherd.desc: Build a Shepherd's Hut to take advantage of those poor unsuspecting sheep
advancements.megamod.build.sifter.descri: Build a Sifter's Hut to take advantage of extra resources hiding in blocks
advancements.megamod.build.sifter.title: Diamond in the Rough
advancements.megamod.build.smeltery.desc: Build a Smeltery to have ore smelted for you! It's pretty efficient with a skilled worker.
advancements.megamod.build.smeltery_3.de: A level 3 Smeltery applies fortune 2 when processing ore blocks, nice!
advancements.megamod.build.smeltery_5.de: A level 5 Smeltery applies fortune 4 when processing ore blocks, can't do better than that!
advancements.megamod.build.stone_smelter: Build a Brick Yard to automate the forging of stone and more for other crafters
advancements.megamod.build.stonemason.de: A Stonemason can craft 3x3 recipes using stone as their main ingredient. Build one to take advantage of their skill!
Bacon - a meat eater's dream. Build a Swineherd's Hut so your citizens don't miss out.
advancements.megamod.build.tavern.descri: Your citizens need a roof over their heads! Build a Tavern for some initial housing. You can also recruit citizens there!
advancements.megamod.build.tavern.title: Any Shelter Will Do
advancements.megamod.build.town_hall.des: Construct the Town Hall. There are only a couple of benefits currently, but it's a cool centerpiece!
advancements.megamod.build.warehouse.des: Need some automation? Build a Warehouse to store materials in a central location!
advancements.megamod.building_add_recipe: Teach your new Carpenter to craft a door. Note: Crafters will only craft when another worker makes a request. See the info tab in their hut for more!
Your Builder can learn 2x2 recipes. Teach them the recipe for a torch and they'll craft it when needed
advancements.megamod.check_out_guide.des: Right-click the Builder's Hut to see how things work
advancements.megamod.citizen.bury.descri: Bury your first citizen. It's a sad day for everyone, but better than letting their grave decay away.
advancements.megamod.citizen.bury.title: A Sad Day Indeed
advancements.megamod.citizen.resurrect.d: Your Undertaker resurrected a fallen citizen!
advancements.megamod.citizen_eat_food_fi: Citizens need to eat to be able to work. Put some food in your citizens' inventories so they can eat it when they need to!
advancements.megamod.citizen_eat_food_ro: So this is what kind of leader you are, feeding your citizens rotten flesh? Shame, shame, shame!
advancements.megamod.click_gui_button_fu: Fulfill one of the Builder's requests for tools or resources using the buttons in their request screen
advancements.megamod.colony_population_1: 10 Citizens means more workers, and more workers means more resources!
advancements.megamod.colony_population_2: 25 Citizens is a lot, hopefully you're keeping them alive!
advancements.megamod.colony_population_5: Time for more citizens! Children may only be born when there is open space in one of the buildings. Children need to be fed and sleep well to grow up.
50 Citizens is enough to have one of each worker - and then some!
advancements.megamod.colony_population_7: You settled. You survived. You thrived.
advancements.megamod.complete_build_requ: Upgrade your Builder's Hut so you can upgrade other huts too!
While there's little benefit to having a level 5 Town Hall, it sure does look pretty!
advancements.megamod.create_build_reques: Craft and place a Builder's Hut with the Build Tool, hire a Builder (automatic by default), and tell them to build their hut!
advancements.megamod.deep_mine.descripti: Have a Miner mine to bedrock
advancements.megamod.deep_mine.title: Rock Bottom
advancements.megamod.max_fields.descript: Assign the maximum number of fields to a single Farmer
advancements.megamod.max_fields.title: Green Thumbs
advancements.megamod.place_townhall.desc: Place the Town Hall using the Build Tool - with this tool you can choose the style and position.
advancements.megamod.post_and_stash.desc: Place a postbox and a stash for easy access to the Warehouse
advancements.megamod.root.description: Craft a Supply Camp or Ship and place it by right-clicking it on the ground
advancements.megamod.root.military.title: Colony Militia
advancements.megamod.root.title: MegaColonies
advancements.megamod.undertaker.totem.de: The Undertaker has been given a Totem of Undying to assist in Resurrection, but make sure you've done your research first!

com.megacolonies.core.gui.reactivate.mes: This once thrived as a %s. With careful attention and the skilled hands of a builder, you can breathe new life into this structure, enabling citizens to once again call it home. Assign a builder to restore it to its former glory, reactivating its purpose and revitalizing the community.

This building once hummed with the activity of a %s. By initiating its reactivation, you'll open doors for citizens to resume their work here. Entrust a skilled builder with its repair, restoring it to its former functionality and igniting productivity once more.

com.megacolonies.core.gui.supplies.guide:
Use these supplies to assemble a %s to serve as a foundation for your own colony. Within it, aside from §2ample resources§r, you shall find
a salvaged §2townhall block§r and §2build tool§r to lay the groundworks of the new colony. Founding a colony demands resilience; beyond the basic necessities of shelter, sustenance, and healing, lies the imperative to fortify against the looming threats of piracy and barbarism. Yet, should you endure these trials, the potential for growth knows no bounds. From artisans to scholars, miners to alchemists, your settlement holds the promise of evolving into a §2self-sustaining bastion of prosperity and knowledge§r.

I come from a neighboring town where I worked as a Carpenter.
I am from a place where the old, dusty books live. But after living so long in between these books, I needed to see other people.
I always wanted to build houses. Big houses, tiny houses, building with wingardium leviosa - but that doesn't seem to work, so I am now resting at your Tavern.
I am an avid guard and while fighting against hordes of zombies, skeletons, and those weird endermen, I heard about a colony which could use a little help against raiders.
I am a treasure hunter. But digging in someone else's trash in search for some gold or iron is very tiring. Now I am looking for another task.
I am quite adept at using swords. If by my life or death I can protect you, I will. You have my sword.'
I am not a fan of this place, but didnt see any others close by.
I am never late, nor early. I always arrive precisely when I meant to.
I like to wander around the world. Wandering makes me hungry and thirsty, so I stopped by your tavern.
I am not the biggest one, but firmly believe that even the smallest person can change the course of the future.
I saw a village with its inhabitants going around in minecarts. It seemed like fun, and maybe I can do that here!
I travel around a lot. A stranger once said 'Chewie, we're home.' I couldn't make much sense of it, though!
*utters weirdly* 'Live long and prosper.'
I was a rich traveling trader until someone robbed me of all my goods.
I was known as a strong fighter in a faraway kingdom, though for some reason I cannot go back there.
I was peacefully living my daily life as a farmer, then I got bitten by a zombie. Thanks for saving me!
com.megacolonies.coremod.gui.chat.shasha: I will always be there for you! Your reliable Shrine Maiden Fox, Caster, has now arrived!
com.megacolonies.coremod.gui.chat.sheize: Hi there! I'm an explorer who goes by the name Reizar. There is something interesting about this place, and I'd like to be a part of it.
com.megacolonies.coremod.gui.chat.sparky: I am a dragonkin from the far northern tundras. I like to study plants and wildlife for use in the arcane arts, but I have also dabbled in this or that over the many years I've lived. Having adventured for quite some time, I might be interested in settling down for a spell...
com.megacolonies.coremod.gui.chat.tobido: I may look scary but I am more of a huggable Bear unless there are Jinchuriki or Spider-Men around but I am also a really good miner. Stick me anywhere, I am here to help Sempai
com.megacolonies.coremod.gui.chat.trazyn: I am the guardian of knowledge and history at Solemnace. I'm now out in the world to search for more knowledge... If you could let me stay with you to learn more of your culture, I would be deeply grateful.
com.megacolonies.coremod.gui.chat.zarlio: Hire me to slaughter your enemies!

com.megacolonies.coremod.info.baker.0: The Baker is part of the food production of your colony. They will bake bread, cookies, pumpkin pie, and/or cake. On page two of the Bakery GUI, you can select which items you want them to make.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.baker.1: The Baker cannot make any food with items that are not consumed in the making like pots and pans. These are usually from mods like Pam's.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.baker.2:
Now let's cover some frequently asked questions:

 Q 1. My Baker is using more items than they are producing, why?
   A 1. The Baker may be consuming some product when they get hungry. Also, while the wheat/other ingredients may not be visible, the product may still be in the process of being produced.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.baker.3:
As all workers do, the Baker levels up while working.
 At higher levels, a Baker's speed increases. The higher their hut level and Intelligence skill, the quicker they level up. 
 However, their level is capped by the level of their home. Thus, if they are unhappy with their living conditions, they will stop leveling until you provide them with better housing.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.barracks.0:
The Barracks is the heart of your military defense. Without it you wouldn't be able to build any Barracks Towers!
 Each Barracks contains four Barracks Towers, one for each level (up to level four).
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.barracks.1:
Now let's cover restrictions:

 1. Barracks only hold 1 Barracks Tower per level, and each tower can only level as high as the Barracks. So if you have a level 1 Barracks, it will only allow 1 Barracks Tower with a max level of 1. If you have a level 3 Barracks, it will allow 3 Barracks Towers with a max level of 3.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.barracks.2:
Now let's cover some frequently asked questions:

 Q 1. Can I have more than 1 Barracks?
   A 1. Yes, but the Barracks Tower levels are based on the Barracks they are attached to. So even if you have one Barracks that is level 5, if the second Barracks is only level 1, then that Barracks can only have a single level 1 tower.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.barracksto:
The Barracks Tower is the heart of your military defense. Each Barracks Tower employs 1 Guard per level for a total of 5. However, the Guards will also live in the tower, freeing up beds for more citizens.
There are two different types of Guards, Knights and Archers. Knights use swords, while Archers use bows.

Now let's cover restrictions:

 1. Guards unlock certain tool levels per their hut level:
   lvl 0. Wood/Gold Tools
   lvl 1. Stone Tools
   lvl 2. Iron Tools
   lvl 3. Diamond Tools
   lvl 4-5. Enchanted Tools

Now let's cover some frequently asked questions:

 Q 1. My Barracks Tower was leveled up, but the Guards aren't asking for higher level equipment. Why?
   A 1. Guards will not ask for updated equipment until they use up their current equipment.

As all workers do, the Guards level up while working.
 At higher levels, a Guard's speed increases. The higher their hut level and Intelligence skill, the quicker they level up. 
 However, their level is capped by the level of their home. Thus, if they are unhappy with their living conditions, they will stop leveling until you provide them with better housing.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.builder.0:
The Builder is the heart of your colony. Without them, you wouldn't be able to get anything done!
 Every hut worker has to have their building built to at least level 1 before they can do anything, except the Builder. If the Builder has a hut at level 0 (no hut), they can only build one thing, their own hut.

 For your Builder to build, upgrade, reposition, or repair a hut that isn't their own, their hut must be at least the same level as the other hut. For example, the Builder's Hut must be level 3 or above to build a level 3 hut.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.builder.1:
Let's cover builder restrictions:

 1. Builders (and most other workers) unlock certain tool levels per their hut level:
   lvl 0. Wood/Gold Tools
   lvl 1. Stone Tools
   lvl 2. Iron Tools
   lvl 3. Diamond Tools
   lvl 4-5. Enchanted Tools

 2. Builders have a maximum range of 100 blocks for buildings for automatic assignment. If your building is outside that range, in the top right of the build options GUI (for the hut you want to be built) you can select which Builder to assign to that building.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.builder.2:
Now let's cover some frequently asked questions:

 Q 1. I'm seeing an item in the required resources page that seems unobtainable or requires Silk Touch to get. What should I do?
   A 1. Things like Air, Dirt Paths, and other blocks will show up in the required resources tab from time to time. While they do show up, focus mainly on your Builder's personal request tab. They'll usually request the correct block and convert it accordingly (e.g. request dirt and then use a shovel on it).
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.builder.3:
As all workers do, the Builder levels up while working.
 At higher levels, the Builder's speed increases. The higher their hut level and Intelligence skill, the quicker they level up. 
 However, their level is capped by the level of their home. Thus, if they are unhappy with their living conditions, they will stop leveling until you provide them with better housing.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.chickenher: The Chicken Farmer is part of the food production of your colony. The Chicken Farmer breeds, raises, and butchers chickens. They will collect eggs, feathers, and meat, as well as any drops from other mods. The Chicken Farmer will keep two chickens alive per level of their hut.

Now let's cover restrictions:

 1. Chicken Farmers unlock certain tool levels per their hut level:
   lvl 0. Wood/Gold Tools
   lvl 1. Stone Tools
   lvl 2. Iron Tools
   lvl 3. Diamond Tools
   lvl 4-5. Enchanted Tools

 2. At least two chickens must be provided for the Chicken Farmer to start breeding and butchering.

Now let's cover some frequently asked questions:

 Q 1. What does my Chicken Farmer need to work?
   A 1. You need to supply at least two chickens to get things started. The Chicken Farmer also needs seeds to breed the chickens and an axe to butcher them.

As all workers do, the Chicken Farmer levels up while working.
 At higher levels, a Chicken Farmer's speed increases. The higher their hut level and Intelligence skill, the quicker they level up. 
 However, their level is capped by the level of their home. Thus, if they are unhappy with their living conditions, they will stop leveling until you provide them with better housing.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.citizen.0: The House is where your citizens will live. For every level of the House, the building will house one citizen. You will need multiple houses to house everyone. The only citizens that will not live in a House are your Guards.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.citizen.1: In order to have children, a male and a female citizen must be housed together in one House. Even if you don't have room in the same hut as them, as long as there is an open bed in any House or Tavern within the colony, a child can be born. If there is an adult in another house, the child will be adopted by them, taking on the last name of that adult. If there is a hut with no adult in it, the child will get a random name.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.citizen.2: Workers have their level capped by the level of their House. Thus, if they are unhappy with their living conditions, they will stop leveling until you provide them with better housing.
The Composter is part of the crafting line of your colony. The Composter will turn items into compost for Farmers and Florists to use.

Now let's cover restrictions:

 1. You must choose which items you want the Composter to use to make compost with. This is found on page two of the hut GUI. 
   Be careful using food items if you have a large colony but not many food production huts!

Now let's cover some frequently asked questions:

 Q 1. Can I use items from other mods?
   A 1. Yes, but these must be added in the MegaColonies configuration file. This is found in (your instance folder)/config/megacolonies-common.toml.

As all workers do, the Composter levels up while working.
 At higher levels, a Composter's speed increases. The higher their hut level and Intelligence skill, the quicker they level up. 
 However, their level is capped by the level of their home. Thus, if they are unhappy with their living conditions, they will stop leveling until you provide them with better housing.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.cook.0:
The Waiter is part of the food production of your colony. The Waiter supplies all your citizens with food and will cook meat and potatoes, as well as other cooked food that only needs a furnace.
 When a citizen gets hungry, they will go to the Cook to get something to eat.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.cook.1:
Now let's cover some frequently asked questions:

 Q 1. The Waiter is asking for items that are already cooked, is this normal?
   A 1. You can provide pre-cooked items for the waiter to distribute to your citizens.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.cook.2:
As all workers do, the Waiter levels up while working.
 At higher levels, a Waiter's speed increases. The higher their hut level and Intelligence skill, the quicker they level up. 
 However, their level is capped by the level of their home. Thus, if they are unhappy with their living conditions, they will stop leveling until you provide them with better housing.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.cowboy.0: The Cowhand is part of the food production of your colony. The Cowhand breeds, raises, and butchers cows. They will collect the meat and leather, as well as any drops from other mods. The Cowhand will keep two cows alive per level of their hut.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.cowboy.1:
Now let's cover restrictions:

 1. Cowhands unlock certain tool levels per their hut level:
   lvl 0. Wood/Gold Tools
   lvl 1. Stone Tools
   lvl 2. Iron Tools
   lvl 3. Diamond Tools
   lvl 4-5. Enchanted Tools

 2. At least two cows must be provided for the Cowhand to start breeding and butchering.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.cowboy.2:
Now let's cover some frequently asked questions:

 Q 1. What does my Cowhand need to work?
   A 1. You need to supply at least two cows to get things started. The Cowhand also needs wheat to breed the cows and an axe to butcher them.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.cowboy.3:
As all workers do, the Cowhand levels up while working.
 At higher levels, a Cowhand's speed increases. The higher their hut level and Intelligence skill, the quicker they level up. 
 However, their level is capped by the level of their home. Thus, if they are unhappy with their living conditions, they will stop leveling until you provide them with better housing.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.deliveryma: Couriers are part of colony management. They are the transportation service of the colony and will pick up unneeded items from worker huts and drop them at the Warehouse, as well as gather needed items from the Warehouse and deliver them to workers.

Now let's cover restrictions:

 The number of stacks a Courier can carry at a time depends on their hut level. 
 Level 1: 1 Stack 
 Level 2: 2 Stacks 
 Level 3: 4 Stacks 
 Level 4: 8 Stacks 
 Level 5: unlimited stacks (that can still fit in their inventory)

Now let's cover some frequently asked questions:

 Q 1. How do I change the priority of who the Couriers pick up from?
   A 1. In each hut, there is a pickup priority setting. 1 is the lowest, 10 is the highest, and there's also never (no items will ever be picked up).

 Q 2. Can I change the delivery priority?
 A 2. No, the delivery schedule is determined by the order the request is sent.

As all workers do, Couriers level up while working.
 At higher levels, a Courier's speed increases. The higher their hut level and Intelligence skill, the quicker they level up. 
 However, their level is capped by the level of their home. Thus, if they are unhappy with their living conditions, they will stop leveling until you provide them with better housing.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.farmer.0: The Farmer is part of the food production of your colony. The Farmer grows crops for the colony. The Farmer can plant and harvest any crop that is planted in farmland, even from other mods.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.farmer.1:
Farmers unlock certain tool levels per their hut level:
   lvl 0. Wood/Gold Tools
   lvl 1. Stone Tools
   lvl 2. Iron Tools
   lvl 3. Diamond Tools
   lvl 4-5. Enchanted Tools

 2. Farmers cannot grow plants that do not use farmland, such as sugar cane, cactus, or bamboo. These are grown by the Planter instead.
 Farmers can only maintain one field per each hut level.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.farmer.2:
Now let's cover some frequently asked questions:

 Q 1. I want a smaller field, how do I tell my Farmer not to grow crops up to the maximum size? 
   A 1. Use the Field GUI to set the size of the field.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.farmer.3:
As all workers do, the Farmer levels up while working.
 At higher levels, a Farmer's speed increases. The higher their hut level and Intelligence skill, the quicker they level up. 
 However, their level is capped by the level of their home. Thus, if they are unhappy with their living conditions, they will stop leveling until you provide them with better housing.
The Fisher is part of the food production of your colony. Simply put, the Fisher will fish as long as you provide them with a fishing rod and a big enough area of water.

Now let's cover restrictions:

 1. The Fisher needs a body of water at least seven blocks by seven blocks and at least one block deep. The hut block must be at the same level as the water and must be <= ten blocks from the water.

Now let's cover some frequently asked questions:

 Q 1. My Fisher is catching more than just fish, why?
   A 1. The Fishermen can catch other items, same as a player can.

As all workers do, the Fisher levels up while working.
 At higher levels, a Fisher's speed increases. The higher their hut level and Intelligence skill, the quicker they level up. 
 However, their level is capped by the level of their home. Thus, if they are unhappy with their living conditions, they will stop leveling until you provide them with better housing.
The Graveyard is the resting place of your fallen citizens!

Without it, you'll still be able to manually collect fallen citizens' graves, but your colony will mourn longer.

Each Graveyard needs an Undertaker, who collects graves within the city.

During their free time, the Undertaker will get to know citizens and visit the Mystical Site, slowly attuning to the mana of the world.

If their Mana skill is high, the Undertaker has a small chance to resurrect a fallen citizen!

You can increase this chance by upgrading the Graveyard, building a Mystical Site, and completing researches in the University.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.guardtower:
The Guard Tower is the basic hut for your military defense. Each Guard Tower employs one Guard. However, the Guard will also live in the tower, freeing up a bed for more citizens.
There are two different types of Guards, Knights and Archers. Knights use swords, while Archers use bows.

Now let's cover restrictions:

 1. Guards unlock certain tool levels per their hut level:
   lvl 0. Wood/Gold Tools
   lvl 1. Stone Tools
   lvl 2. Iron Tools
   lvl 3. Diamond Tools
   lvl 4-5. Enchanted Tools

Now let's cover some frequently asked questions:

 Q 1. My Guard Tower was leveled up, but the Guard isn't asking for higher-level equipment. Why?
   A 1. Guards will not ask for updated equipment until they use up their current equipment.

As all workers do, the Guard levels up while working.
 At higher levels, a Guard's speed increases. The higher their hut level and Intelligence skill, the quicker they level up. 
 However, their level is capped by the level of their home. Thus, if they are unhappy with their living conditions, they will stop leveling until you provide them with better housing.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.lumberjack:
The Forester is one of the production workers for your colony. Give them an axe and they'll cut down any trees around them. They'll also replant a sapling where the tree was if they have any.
 If you wish to apply restrictions on what trees they can or can't cut, take a look at the second page.
 Even cooler is the third page. Here you can tell the Forester to not replant saplings and tell them to be restricted to a certain 3D box (selected with the tool given by the third button on the page).

Now let's cover restrictions:

 1. Foresters unlock certain tool levels per their hut level:
   lvl 0. Wood/Gold Tools
   lvl 1. Stone Tools
   lvl 2. Iron Tools
   lvl 3. Diamond Tools
   lvl 4-5. Enchanted Tools

 2. Foresters are able to search for trees within 150 blocks of them (not their hut). While searching, they start with a 50 block range and then increase that range by 5 if they do not find a tree.

3. The definition of a "Tree" in Minecraft is pretty hazy at best. We've done our best to define a tree, but in some cases it may not be enough. Generally, we define a tree as a log and leaves next to each other. Due to this, some of your huts or decorations may be affected by the Forester. To counter this, you can place a piece of cobblestone below a log to mark it as "not a tree".

As all workers do, Foresters level up while working.
 At higher levels, the Forester's speed increases. The higher their hut level and Intelligence skill, the quicker they level up. 
 However, their level is capped by the level of their home. Thus, if they are unhappy with their living conditions, they will stop leveling until you provide them with better housing.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.miner.0: The Miner is one of the production workers for your colony. They will need a pickaxe to mine stone, a shovel to mine dirt and gravel, and sometimes even an axe if they run into a mineshaft. They will also need supplies to build their own mineshafts, such as blocks, slabs, fences/walls, signs, ladders, and torches.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.miner.1:
Let's cover restrictions:

 1. Miners unlock certain tool levels per their hut level:
   lvl 0. Wood/Gold Tools
   lvl 1. Stone Tools
   lvl 2. Iron Tools
   lvl 3. Diamond Tools
   lvl 4-5. Enchanted Tools
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.miner.2:
Now let's cover some frequently asked questions:

 Q 1. How far down will my miner dig before making nodes?
   A 1. Without intervention, at level 1 they will build nodes after they hit y level 50, at level 2 they will make nodes after they hit y level 30, and at level 3 they will make nodes after they hit y level 5.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.miner.3:
Q2. My Miner just stops working or wanders around above ground, why?
  A 2. Either something is blocking their path or they don't have the necessary materials. See page 1 for the materials they need or check the Miner's request page.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.miner.4:
As all workers do, Miners level up while working.
 At higher levels, the Miner's speed increases. The higher their hut level and Intelligence skill, the quicker they level up. 
 However, their level is capped by the level of their home. Thus, if they are unhappy with their living conditions, they will stop leveling until you provide them with better housing.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.shepherd.0: The Shepherd is part of the food production of your colony. The Shepherd breeds, raises, shears, and butchers sheep. They will collect the meat and wool, as well as any drops from other mods. The Shepherd will keep two sheep alive per level of their hut. If you choose, on the second page of the GUI you can tell the Shepherd to randomly dye sheep to get colored wool.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.shepherd.1:
Now let's cover restrictions:

 1. Shepherds unlock certain tool levels per their hut level:
   lvl 0. Wood/Gold Tools
   lvl 1. Stone Tools
   lvl 2. Iron Tools
   lvl 3. Diamond Tools
   lvl 4-5. Enchanted Tools

 2. At least two sheep must be provided for the Shepherd to start breeding and butchering.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.shepherd.2:
Now let's cover some frequently asked questions:

 Q 1. What does my Shepherd need to work?
   A 1. You need to supply at least two sheep to get things started. The Shepherd also needs wheat to breed the sheep, shears to shear the wool, and an axe to butcher the sheep.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.shepherd.3:
As all workers do, the Shepherd levels up while working.
 At higher levels, a Shepherd's speed increases. The higher their hut level and Intelligence skill, the quicker they level up. 
 However, their level is capped by the level of their home. Thus, if they are unhappy with their living conditions, they will stop leveling until you provide them with better housing.
com.megacolonies.coremod.info.swineherde: The Swineherd is part of the food production of your colony. The Swineherd breeds, raises, and butchers pigs. They will collect the meat, as well as any drops from other mods. The Swineherd will keep two pigs alive per level of their hut.

Now let's cover restrictions:

 1. Swineherds unlock certain tool levels per their hut level:
   lvl 0. Wood/Gold Tools
   lvl 1. Stone Tools
   lvl 2. Iron Tools
   lvl 3. Diamond Tools
   lvl 4-5. Enchanted Tools

 2. At least two pigs must be provided for the Swineherd to start breeding and butchering.

Now let's cover some frequently asked questions:

 Q 1. What does my Swineherd need to work?
   A 1. You need to supply at least two pigs to get things started. The Swineherd also needs carrots to breed the pigs and an axe to butcher them.

As all workers do, the Swineherd levels up while working.
 At higher levels, a Swineherd's speed increases. The higher their hut level and Intelligence skill, the quicker they level up. 
 However, their level is capped by the level of their home. Thus, if they are unhappy with their living conditions, they will stop leveling until you provide them with better housing.
The Warehouse is part of colony management. It's where all of your excess supplies are stored. There are no workers assigned to the Warehouse, but Couriers use the Warehouse.

Let's cover restrictions:

 1. Space is limited to the number of racks in the schematic and you can not add your own. If you do, they will get destroyed when the building is updated and the Couriers will not see/use those racks. You also cannot move the racks already in the building. If you break a rack, it is no longer seen by the Couriers.

3. Now let's cover some frequently asked questions: 

 Q 1. I need more storage space that is given, how do I get more? 
 A 1. Upgrade the building. If the building is level 5, you can increase the space in each rack by buying more on page 2 of the GUI using an emerald block. This can be done 3 times.

Q 2. I want to move my Warehouse, can I do that? 
 A 2. You can, but if there is anything in the racks, the contents will spill out when the racks are removed by the Builder. If you must move the Warehouse, remove all the contents from the racks first, and watch to make sure any Couriers don't deliver items.

event.megamod.raidmessage_u1: A mysterious sunken shipwreck has been been spotted to the %s of %s! It is a small sloop, you should consider destroying it.
event.megamod.raidmessage_u2: Citizens report rumors of drowned pirates closing in %s of %s! There also seems to be a sunk ship there, better destroy it.
event.megamod.raidmessage_u3: Arrglglglgl! There's some darn drowned pirates from the %s comin' for %s tonight! There is even a sunk ship 'ere and will continue attacking until we destroy their caravel.
event.megamod.raidmessage_u4: A huge horde of drowned pirates are swimming' %s of %s and they're not here to play! Their rigorous waves will hit us unless we destroy their sunken galleon!

megacolonies.config.colonyteamborders.co: When true, the colony borders shown when holding the build tool will be in the colony's team color. When false, the colony you're inside will be white and any other colony will be red.

megacolonies.config.crusherproduction.co: List of materials a Crusher can reduce and what they convert into, separated by exclamation marks.

The amount of ticks between retries of the request system for retryable requests. Lower increases server load.
megacolonies.config.dirtfromcompost.comm: Quantity of dirt per compost filling.
megacolonies.config.disableemptycolonies: Will stop empty colonies from being generated in the world - Needs world Reload

megacolonies.config.dynamictreeharvestsi: Forester harvest trunk size for dynamic trees: 1-8.

megacolonies.config.enableindevelopmentf: Should development features be enabled (might be buggy)?
megacolonies.config.enchantments.comment: The possible enchantments for the enchanter.
megacolonies.config.fishingroddurability: Offset for the maximum durability unenchanted rod a T1 Fishing Hut can use, compared to other wooden tools (59). Vanilla rods always work, but equivalents would need +6. Default value allows up to Thermal Foundation Silver  Anything below -59 will only allow vanilla rods.

Offset for the maximum durability unenchanted rod a T2 Fishing Hut can use, compared to iron tools (250). Thermal Foundation Iron requires +6. T3 huts can use unenchanted rods of any durability level, so long as they can take damage at all. Anything below -250 will only allow vanilla rods, except as allowed by T1.
megacolonies.config.forceloadcolony.comm: If part of the colony is loaded by an owner/officer, should the colony be kept loaded? (Set how many chunks are loaded with the "Colony Chunk Loading Strictness" option.)
megacolonies.config.freetointeractblocks: Blocks players should be able to interact with in any colony (ex: vending machines)
megacolonies.config.gameplay.comment: All configuration items related to the core gameplay
megacolonies.config.generatesupplyloot.c: Whether to generate supply ships and camps in loot chests.
megacolonies.config.holidayfeatures.comm: Should special holiday content be displayed?
megacolonies.config.initialcolonysize.co: Initial claim size for a colony. This is the radius, measured in chunks.
megacolonies.config.listofplantables.com: The items and item-tags that the Florist can plant.
megacolonies.config.loadtime.comment: Set how long chunks stay loaded after player leaves, does not persist through restarts. Default: 10min
megacolonies.config.luckyblockchance.com: Chance for the Miner to get an ore when mining cobblestone or stone (by default, can be expanded with datapacks to other materials). This is a percentage. (To change which ores the Miner can find, see "List of Lucky Ores".)
megacolonies.config.luckyores.comment: List of ore blocks rarely found magically by Miners (see "Ore Chance"). Includes the block and its chance, separated by exclamation marks.
megacolonies.config.maxRaiders.comment: The maximum amount of raiders spawned per raid
megacolonies.config.maxcolonysize.commen: Maximum claim range for a colony. This is the radius, measured in chunks.

megacolonies.config.maximalretries.comme: The maximal amount of tries that the request system will perform for retryable requests. Higher increases server load.
megacolonies.config.maxitemsubtypescan.c: Max amount of items we scan of a given subtype before we stop.
megacolonies.config.maxkeptbackups.comme: Set the max amount of backups kept
megacolonies.config.maxtreesize.comment: Max log count in one tree for the Forester to check during their tree search.
megacolonies.config.maxyforbarbarians.co: Max Y level (height) for Raiders to spawn.
megacolonies.config.mincolonydistance.co: The minimum distance (in chunks) between colonies.
megacolonies.config.mindistancefromworld: Min distance (in blocks) from world spawn for a colony.
